title: quercus dankiaensis
intro: Quercus dankiaensis, also known as the Dannaka or Dannak oak, is a species of tree in the family Fagaceae, native to the northeastern parts of India and Bangladesh. The medicinal properties of Quercus dankiaensis are attributed to its bark, leaves, and roots, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, manage diabetes, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. The herbal preparations of Quercus dankiaensis include decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to create medicines, tonics, and other health supplements.
